slices.task: This is a single-slice terminal mission. Do the following and write everything to artifacts/missions/terminal-brief-execute-20260429-0756/sentinel-terminal-brief.md: (1) Run sqlite3 /Users/ryleebenson/Desktop/OPENCLAW/data/crm.sqlite 'SELECT status, COUNT(*) FROM email_attempts GROUP BY status' and report the full ledger. (2) Run sqlite3 ... 'SELECT stage, COUNT(*) FROM leads GROUP BY stage' and report pipeline stages. (3) Run sqlite3 ... 'SELECT l.name,l.score,l.stage,e.status FROM leads l LEFT JOIN email_attempts e ON l.id=e.lead_id WHERE l.stage="outreach_sent" ORDER BY l.score DESC LIMIT 10' and report top sent leads. (4) curl outboundautonomy.com/ /try /sample-report /pricing /case-studies /faq /blog and report all HTTP codes. (5) In the file, include a section 'OPERATOR NEXT ACTION' with exactly: '91 outreach emails sent via SMTP with provider_accepted evidence. Reply window: 24-72 hours. If any prospect replies, operator must respond personally (RED per BOOT.md — replying to leads). If no replies by April 30 evening, authorize follow-up batch. Open tickets: waitlist API (ORANGE — needs Vercel log access), CDP browser down (YELLOW), CRM Python fd error (YELLOW — sqlite3 fallback works).' (6) End the file with 'CYCLE CLOSED — awaiting operator direction or material pipeline change.' This is the final output — no further action expected.

reasoning: 15 proposals submitted. 91 sends out. The pipeline is producing. The operator needs a close-out, not another proposal. This mission produces exactly one artifact — a terminal brief with the full pipeline snapshot and a clear next-action directive. It delegates to auditor (single slice) to produce the verified brief.
